rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 25, 2014, Line 1, Compliance Hearing - Order To Show Cause Re Contempt (John Giddings) Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD FRAIGE VS. MATTHEW T. KESSLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Matthew Kesslers Further Responses To Requests For Production Nos. 111 And 112 And For Further Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 25, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF RICHARD FRAIGE's Motion To Compel Defendant Matthew Kesslers Further Responses To Requests For Production Nos. 111 And 112 And For Further Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The tax returns are also privileged under the privacy privilege afforded by Cal. Const. Art 1, Sec 1, and court does not find facts sufficient to support a waiver of the privilege by the defendant. Counsel's offer in correspondence does not suffice for a knowing waiver by the defendant. Further, moving party has not demonstrated sufficient necessity to overcome the privilege. Tax returns are not dispositive on the issue whether under California law the defendant was an employee or independent contractor. The issuance of W-2 forms or 1099 forms by the plaintiff would be in the control and custody of the plaintiff in any event. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KERRI NAU VS. SLOANE NIGHT CLUB, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 25, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT CARLOS RAMOS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion is denied. Conversion can support plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. See Ferraro v. Pacific Fin. Corp. (1970) 8 Cal.App.3d 339, 351 ("Exemplary damages are properly awardable in an action for conversion, given the required showing of malice, fraud, or oppression.") (citation omitted). Paragraphs 58, 60, 63, 64 of the conversion cause of action allege that Ramos "grabbed" Nau's cell phone with the intent to keep and convert it to his own use and possession. Paragraphs 59, 63, 64 allege that Ramos took Nau's wallet and converted it to his own possession and use. These allegations of an intentional tort are taken as true and support the claim for punitives. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RUBEN TORRES VS. BENIHANA NATIONAL CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 25, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF RUBEN TORRES' Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted as to special interrogatories numbers 8, 9, and 32. The court considers that the requested information is limited as to time and place, being a four year period and only with reference to the plaintiff's work shifts. As to the privacy objections on behalf of the employees, while the privilege applies to the contact information for any employee, subject to an opt out notice, it does not apply to a refusal to disclose the names. Respondent party has not met its burden to demonstrate equal availability of the information under CCP Secs. 2030.220(c), 2030.230. Defendant's supplemental response to special interrogatory number 8 does not respond to the interrogatory. The other objections are not found to be substantially justified. Request for sanctions granted in the amount of $1,500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG K MARTIN VS. DOES 1-50, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 25, 2014, Line 15: DEFENDANTS MARK TORRES-GIL, STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, LUCY ARMENDARIZ, SHERRIE MCLETCHIE, ROBERT HENDERSON, WONDER LIANG, SUSAN CHAN, DONALD STEEDMAN, ROBERT ENDRIES, LAWRENCE DAL CERRO, ALLEN BLUMENTHAL, RUSSELL WEINER, MIKE NISPEROS, JR., and SCOTT DREXEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity¿If the court finds such a showing has been made¿it then determines whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) In determining whether the allegations arise from a protected activity, the court's "focus is on the principal thrust or gravamen of the causes of action, i.e., the allegedly wrongful and injury producing conduct that provides the foundation for the claims." (Castleman v. Sagaser (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 481, 490-91.) Here, each of Plaintiff's causes of action arises out of the State Bar disciplinary proceedings brought against him or the defense of the federal suit filed by Plaintiff. Thus, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute is met. Plaintiff has not demonstrated a probability of prevailing. A plaintiff is required to "show both that the claim is legally sufficient and there is admissible evidence that, if credited, would be sufficient to sustain a favorable judgment." (McGarry v. University of San Diego 154 Cal.App.4th 97, 108.) The only evidence submitted by Plaintiff are the unanswered requests for admission that Plaintiff propounded to various Defendants. This is insufficient to demonstrate a probability of prevailing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BABAK DOUSTKAM VS. LINDA CHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 25, 2014, Line 11: DEFENDANT LINDA CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to breach of contract. Plaintiff apparently intended but failed to include a copy of a written agreement as an exhibit to the Complaint. Leave to amend in order to attach the agreement is granted. Demurrer to this cause of action on all other grounds is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged each element of a breach of contract claim. Demurrer is overruled as to the defamation and defamation per se causes of action. The Complaint quotes no less than seven allegedly false statements, (Compl. 25-27, 29, 30, 31, 33) and sufficiently alleges each required element. Moreover, the Complaint contains allegations of statements made after March 13, 2012 and therefore the demurrer is overruled as the later statements support the cause of action. Demurrer is sustained as to the unfair business practices cause of action with fifteen days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to allege an injury in fact and a causal connection between Defendant's UCL violation and Plaintiff's injury. (See Bus. & Prof. Code § 17204 as amended by Prop 64.) Demurrer to this cause of action on all other grounds is overruled. Demurrer is also overruled as to the intentional infliction of emotion distress cause of action as the Complaint sufficiently alleges each element. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to the prohibitory injunction claim. A Complaint may state a cause of action for injunctive relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BABAK DOUSTKAM VS. LINDA CHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 25, 2014, Line 11: DEFENDANT LINDA CHAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to Strike is granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to striking Los Angeles County as the proper venue. Defendant fails to present and the Court fails to identify any reason to grant, and finds the request entirely unnecessary. Motion denied as to the prayer for injunctive relief. Defamation is not constitutionally protected speech. Motion is granted as to Plaintiff's requests for attorneys' fees with fifteen days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to state the contractual or statutory basis for the request. Motion denied as to striking punitive damages as the Complaint sufficiently alleges facts amounting to malice and an intent to injure Plaintiff. Motion denied as to striking the defamation cause of action as duplicative of defamation per se. Plaintiff is permitted to assert alternative causes of action. Motion is granted without leave to amend as to the allegations of defamatory statements made prior to March 13, 2012 as barred by the statute of limitations, which is not a curable defect. Motion is denied as to striking the unfair business practices cause of action because it is not merely duplicative of the defamation claims. Motion denied as to striking the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Defendant sets forth grounds for demurrer to a cause of action rather than grounds to strike. Finally, the Motion is denied as to striking the preliminary injunction cause of action. Defendant failed to explain in what way the cause of action does not conform to the applicable rules of court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
